subject: general-knowledge
description: Economic Blocs & Global Organizations MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What does BRICS stand for?
options: Britain, Russia, India, China, South Africa
Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa
Brazil, Romania, Iran, China, South Korea
mcqs.explanation: BRICS = Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa. South Africa joined in 2010, making BRIC → BRICS.

mcqs.q: Who coined the term 'BRIC' in 2001?
mcqs.explanation: Jim O'Neill, Chief Economist at Goldman Sachs, coined the term BRIC in his 2001 paper 'Building Better Global Economic BRICs'.

mcqs.q: Which countries were the initial BRIC members (2001–2009)?
mcqs.explanation: The original BRIC group was Brazil, Russia, India and China — the four largest emerging-market economies identified by Goldman Sachs.

mcqs.q: Which country joined in 2010 to make BRIC → BRICS?
mcqs.explanation: South Africa was formally invited to join the bloc in December 2010, adding the 'S' to make BRICS.

mcqs.q: Where was the first BRIC Foreign Ministers' meeting held in 2006?
mcqs.explanation: The first BRIC Foreign Ministers' meeting was held on the sidelines of the UN General Assembly in New York in September 2006.

mcqs.q: Where did the first BRIC Summit take place on 16 June 2009?
mcqs.explanation: The first BRIC leaders' summit was held in Yekaterinburg, Russia, on 16 June 2009.

mcqs.q: What is a primary goal of BRICS?
mcqs.explanation: BRICS' core goal is to promote a multipolar world order and amplify the voice of the Global South in international governance.

mcqs.q: As of 2025, how many members does BRICS have?
mcqs.explanation: After the January 2024 expansion, BRICS has 10 members: the original 5 plus Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, UAE and Saudi Arabia.

mcqs.q: Which of the following became new members of BRICS on 1 January 2024?
options: Saudi Arabia, Iran, Egypt, Ethiopia and UAE
Pakistan, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal, Bhutan
mcqs.explanation: Five new members joined on 1 January 2024: Saudi Arabia, Iran, Egypt, Ethiopia and the UAE.

mcqs.q: Which country declined to join BRICS after a change in government in late 2023?
mcqs.explanation: Argentina was invited but its new president Javier Milei declined membership in December 2023.

mcqs.q: BRICS members collectively represent approximately what percentage of the world population?
mcqs.explanation: Expanded BRICS-10 represents about 45% of the global population.

mcqs.q: What percentage of Earth's land area does the BRICS bloc cover?
mcqs.explanation: BRICS-10 covers about 27% of Earth's total land area.

mcqs.q: True or False: All BRICS countries are members of the G20.
mcqs.explanation: All five original BRICS members are part of the G20. Several new members (Saudi Arabia) are also G20 members.

mcqs.q: BRICS countries are generally characterized as:
mcqs.explanation: BRICS is the leading bloc of developing and newly industrialised emerging-market economies.

mcqs.q: What institution did BRICS create in 2014, headquartered in Shanghai?
mcqs.explanation: The New Development Bank (NDB) was founded by BRICS in 2014 and is headquartered in Shanghai.

mcqs.q: How does the NDB differ from the IMF and World Bank?
mcqs.explanation: Unlike the IMF/World Bank, the NDB issues development loans without political conditionalities.

mcqs.q: Who was the first President of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
mcqs.explanation: K. V. Kamath of India was the first President of the NDB (2015–2020).

mcqs.q: What is the BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement (CRA) worth?
mcqs.explanation: The CRA, established in 2014, is a $100 billion liquidity safety net for BRICS members.

mcqs.q: What is the purpose of the BRICS Business Council?
mcqs.explanation: The BRICS Business Council brings together private sector leaders to promote trade, innovation and cross-border investment.

mcqs.q: When was the BRICS Parliamentary Forum established to foster legislative cooperation?
mcqs.explanation: The BRICS Parliamentary Forum was institutionalised in 2022 to support legislative-level cooperation.

mcqs.q: What system is under development by BRICS to replace SWIFT?
mcqs.explanation: BRICS is developing the BRICS Payment System to bypass dependence on Western-controlled SWIFT.

mcqs.q: What is the proposed common digital currency to counter dollar dominance often referred to as?
mcqs.explanation: A proposed BRICS digital currency — informally dubbed BRICoin — is being discussed to reduce reliance on the US dollar.

mcqs.q: In 2024, trade in local currencies among BRICS members exceeded what amount?
mcqs.explanation: Local-currency trade among BRICS states surpassed $150 billion in 2024 as part of de-dollarisation efforts.

mcqs.q: Which forum promotes digital transformation and cybersecurity within the bloc?
mcqs.explanation: The BRICS Information Technology Forum coordinates digital-transformation and cybersecurity policy across the bloc.

mcqs.q: What was established in 2022 to strengthen pandemic cooperation?
mcqs.explanation: The BRICS Vaccine Research and Development Center was launched in 2022 to coordinate pandemic preparedness.

mcqs.q: What platform invests in solar, wind and hydrogen projects?
mcqs.explanation: The BRICS Green Energy Platform channels investment into renewables — solar, wind and green hydrogen.

mcqs.q: The BRICS Space Cooperation Agreement (2021) supports what?
mcqs.explanation: The 2021 BRICS Space Cooperation Agreement pools satellite data for climate and disaster monitoring.

mcqs.q: BRICS countries jointly oppose which of the following?
options: Unilateral sanctions, protectionism and military intervention
mcqs.explanation: BRICS regularly issues joint declarations opposing unilateral sanctions, trade protectionism and unilateral military intervention.

mcqs.q: What reform do BRICS countries advocate for to give the Global South greater representation?
mcqs.explanation: BRICS strongly advocates UN Security Council reform — particularly permanent seats for Brazil, India and South Africa.

mcqs.q: BRICS promotes which concept for developing states regarding the environment?
mcqs.explanation: BRICS champions 'climate justice' — equitable burden-sharing reflecting historical emissions by developed economies.

mcqs.q: Which country is the largest economy in BRICS, contributing 70%+ of its GDP growth?
mcqs.explanation: China is the largest BRICS economy and contributes more than 70% of the bloc's GDP growth.

mcqs.q: Which country relies on BRICS for trade diversification due to sanctions since 2022?
mcqs.explanation: Russia, under sweeping Western sanctions since 2022, has used BRICS to diversify trade and payment links.

mcqs.q: Which BRICS member plays a balancing role, maintaining ties with Western and Eastern blocs?
mcqs.explanation: India follows a strategic-autonomy doctrine, balancing relations with the US/Western bloc and Russia/China.

mcqs.q: Which country represents Latin America in BRICS, emphasizing sustainable agriculture?
mcqs.explanation: Brazil is the bloc's Latin American voice and uses BRICS to advance sustainable-agriculture priorities.

mcqs.q: Which member acts as the gateway to Africa, hosting summits in 2013 and 2023?
mcqs.explanation: South Africa hosted BRICS summits in Durban (2013) and Johannesburg (2023) — the bloc's African gateway.

mcqs.q: Which new member's entry made BRICS a global oil hub, controlling ~44% of oil output?
mcqs.explanation: Saudi Arabia's accession (along with Iran, UAE) gave BRICS control over ~44% of global oil output.

mcqs.q: Which new member strengthened BRICS' anti-sanction stance and energy security?
mcqs.explanation: Iran's BRICS entry strengthened the bloc's anti-sanctions agenda and energy-security profile.

mcqs.q: Which new member expanded BRICS influence in finance and logistics?
mcqs.explanation: The UAE — a global logistics and financial hub — broadens BRICS' presence in trade and finance.

mcqs.q: Which new member boosted African representation and trade links?
mcqs.explanation: Egypt's accession bolstered Africa's voice and boosted Mediterranean/African trade access in BRICS.

mcqs.q: Which new member aligned BRICS with the African Union agenda?
mcqs.explanation: Ethiopia (host of the African Union HQ in Addis Ababa) aligns BRICS more closely with the AU agenda.

mcqs.q: Which summit finalized BRICS' first expansion since 2010?
mcqs.explanation: The 15th BRICS Summit in Johannesburg (August 2023) finalized invitations to the five new members.

mcqs.q: The Johannesburg II Declaration (2023) reaffirmed support for what cause?
mcqs.explanation: The Johannesburg II Declaration reaffirmed support for Palestinian statehood and a two-state solution.

mcqs.q: Which country holds the 2024–25 Chairmanship and hosted the Kazan Summit (2025)?
mcqs.explanation: Russia chaired BRICS during 2024–25 and hosted the Kazan Summit in 2024.

mcqs.q: In 2024, BRICS celebrated how many years of summits?
mcqs.explanation: BRICS marked 15 years of summits in 2024 (first summit was Yekaterinburg, 2009).

mcqs.q: Is Pakistan a member of BRICS?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan is not a BRICS member but publicly supports BRICS+ expansion and de-dollarisation efforts.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's stance towards BRICS aligns with efforts for South–South cooperation and what else?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan supports BRICS goals of South–South cooperation and de-dollarisation in global trade.

mcqs.q: Pakistan's interest in BRICS is tied to its membership in which other organization?
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's BRICS interest is closely tied to its full membership in the SCO (Shanghai Cooperation Organization).

mcqs.q: How did Pakistan react to the BRICS expansion in 2023?
options: Welcomed it as a move toward inclusive global governance
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan officially welcomed the BRICS expansion as a step toward more inclusive global governance.

mcqs.q: In terms of GDP (PPP), which group has BRICS surpassed?
mcqs.explanation: In PPP terms, BRICS-10 has now surpassed the G7's combined GDP — a major geopolitical milestone.

mcqs.q: Approximately how much do BRICS members hold in foreign reserves?
mcqs.explanation: Combined BRICS foreign-exchange reserves total roughly $4.5 trillion — among the world's largest.

mcqs.q: What global market is significantly influenced collectively by the expanded BRICS?
mcqs.explanation: With Saudi Arabia, Iran and UAE inside, BRICS now exerts decisive influence over global energy (oil and gas) markets.

mcqs.q: What does BRICS symbolize in geopolitics?
options: A new multipolar world order centered in the Global South
mcqs.explanation: Strategically, BRICS symbolizes a new multipolar world order led by emerging economies of the Global South.

mcqs.q: What defines an 'Agency' in a global context?
options: An organization created to perform specific functions or implement policies
mcqs.explanation: A global Agency is an organization created with a specific mandate to perform functions or implement policy (e.g. UN specialized agencies).

mcqs.q: What is a 'Group' (like G-7 or G-20)?
options: An informal alliance of countries formed for dialogue without binding rules
mcqs.explanation: Groups such as G-7 / G-20 are informal alliances for dialogue and coordination — they have no binding charter or treaty.

mcqs.q: What characterizes an 'Organization' (like the UN)?
options: A formal intergovernmental body created through a treaty or charter
mcqs.explanation: Organizations like the UN are formal intergovernmental bodies created by an international treaty or charter.

mcqs.q: What is an 'Alliance' (e.g., NATO)?
options: A strategic or military partnership based on mutual defense/security
mcqs.explanation: An Alliance such as NATO is a strategic and military partnership built on mutual defense/security obligations.

mcqs.q: What describes a 'Community' (e.g., Caribbean Community)?
options: A cooperative association sharing cultural, political or economic interests
mcqs.explanation: A Community such as CARICOM is a cooperative association of states sharing cultural, political or economic interests.

mcqs.q: What is a 'Union' (like the European Union)?
options: A highly integrated organization involving shared governance or policies
mcqs.explanation: A Union like the EU is a deeply integrated body with shared governance, laws, currency and policies.

mcqs.q: What is a 'Bloc' (e.g., Eastern Bloc)?
options: A cluster of countries acting together for common political/economic objectives
mcqs.explanation: A Bloc is a cluster of countries acting together for shared political or economic objectives.

mcqs.q: What is a 'Forum' (e.g., APEC)?
options: A platform for dialogue where members make non-binding decisions
mcqs.explanation: A Forum such as APEC is a non-binding dialogue platform — decisions are recommendations, not treaties.

mcqs.q: What defines a 'Coalition'?
options: A temporary or issue-specific partnership to pursue shared actions
mcqs.explanation: A Coalition is a temporary, issue-specific partnership formed to pursue a shared action (e.g. war-time coalitions).

mcqs.q: What is a 'Region / Regional Term' (like MENA)?
options: A geographical/corporate label describing a collection of states by location
mcqs.explanation: A regional term like MENA (Middle East & North Africa) describes a group of states by geography or business taxonomy.

mcqs.q: What are 'Movements' (like NAM)?
options: Ideological or political campaigns representing collective aspirations
mcqs.explanation: Movements such as the Non-Aligned Movement are ideological or political campaigns representing collective aspirations of states or peoples.

mcqs.q: Which organization was founded in 2002 uniting 55 African states, headquartered in Addis Ababa?
mcqs.explanation: The African Union (AU) was founded in 2002 (succeeding the OAU), uniting 55 African states, with HQ in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.

mcqs.q: What does AUB stand for?
mcqs.explanation: AUB stands for the African Union of Broadcasting — coordinating broadcasters across the continent.

mcqs.q: What regions does 'AMEA' represent?
options: Asia, the Middle East and Africa
mcqs.explanation: AMEA is a corporate/regional grouping standing for Asia, the Middle East and Africa.

mcqs.q: The term AMER or AMERS collectively denotes which regions?
mcqs.explanation: AMER / AMERS is shorthand for the Americas — both North and South America together.

mcqs.q: Which regional economic organization joins Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador and Peru?
mcqs.explanation: The Andean Community (CAN) is a customs union of Bolivia, Colombia, Ecuador and Peru.

mcqs.q: What does ANZ represent?
mcqs.explanation: ANZ is a common abbreviation for Australia and New Zealand.

mcqs.q: ANZIT is a trilateral diplomatic group linking Australia, New Zealand and which other country?
mcqs.explanation: ANZIT links Australia, New Zealand and Italy in a diplomatic dialogue framework.

mcqs.q: ANZUK was a short-lived defense alliance (1971–1975) of Australia, New Zealand and:
mcqs.explanation: ANZUK was a 1971–1975 defence pact among Australia, New Zealand and the United Kingdom.

mcqs.q: What regions does APAC refer to?
options: East, South and Southeast Asia plus Oceania
mcqs.explanation: APAC stands for Asia-Pacific — East, South, Southeast Asia and Oceania.

mcqs.q: APEC unites 21 Pacific-rim economies to promote what?
mcqs.explanation: APEC (Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation) unites 21 Pacific-rim economies to promote trade liberalization and inclusive growth.

mcqs.q: Founded in 1945 in Cairo, which organization comprises 22 Arab states?
mcqs.explanation: The Arab League (League of Arab States) was founded in Cairo in 1945 and currently has 22 Arab member states.

mcqs.q: Which movement inspired the creation of the Arab League and Ba'ath ideology?
mcqs.explanation: The Arab Nationalist Movement inspired both the Arab League and the Ba'ath ideology of secular Arab unity.

mcqs.q: Which union formed in 1989 unites Algeria, Libya, Mauritania, Morocco and Tunisia?
mcqs.explanation: The Arab Maghreb Union (AMU) was founded in 1989 to integrate Algeria, Libya, Mauritania, Morocco and Tunisia.

mcqs.q: Where is ASEAN headquartered?
mcqs.explanation: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) is headquartered in Jakarta, Indonesia.

mcqs.q: ASEAN Plus Three (ASEAN+3) includes ASEAN and which three countries?
mcqs.explanation: ASEAN+3 adds China, Japan and South Korea to ASEAN.

mcqs.q: ASEAN Plus Six expands cooperation by adding which countries to the Plus Three?
mcqs.explanation: ASEAN+6 adds India, Australia and New Zealand to ASEAN+3.

mcqs.q: What is ACD?
mcqs.explanation: ACD = Asian Cooperation Dialogue, an inter-governmental forum of Asian states promoting regional cooperation.

mcqs.q: What does the Assembly of European Regions (AER) do?
options: Facilitates cooperation between sub-national regional governments
mcqs.explanation: The AER facilitates cooperation among sub-national regional governments across Europe.

mcqs.q: Which organization promotes collaboration among Caribbean and Central American nations?
mcqs.explanation: The Association of Caribbean States (ACS) promotes collaboration among Caribbean and Central American nations.

mcqs.q: AUKUS is a defense alliance between Australia, the UK and the US formed in what year?
mcqs.explanation: AUKUS — covering nuclear-powered submarines and advanced defence tech — was announced in September 2021.

mcqs.q: Which global campaign mobilized international pressure to end racial segregation in South Africa?
mcqs.explanation: The Anti-Apartheid Movement was a global campaign that pressured governments and companies to end apartheid in South Africa.

mcqs.q: What was the Asian Socialist Movement?
options: A movement promoting democratic socialism and anti-colonial solidarity after WWII
mcqs.explanation: The Asian Socialist Movement was a post-WWII movement that promoted democratic socialism and Asian anti-colonial solidarity.

mcqs.q: The Balkan States refer to countries in which part of Europe?
mcqs.explanation: Balkan States are in Southeastern Europe — including Serbia, Bulgaria, Croatia, North Macedonia, Albania, Bosnia and Greece.

mcqs.q: Which countries make up the Baltic States?
mcqs.explanation: The Baltic States are Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania — all NATO and EU members.

mcqs.q: Which countries form the BASIC group advocating for equitable climate-change agreements?
options: Burma, Australia, Sri Lanka, India, Canada
mcqs.explanation: BASIC = Brazil, South Africa, India, China — a climate-negotiation bloc within the UNFCCC.

mcqs.q: The Benelux Union was a precursor to European integration involving Belgium, the Netherlands and:
mcqs.explanation: The Benelux Union joined Belgium, Netherlands and Luxembourg — a forerunner of the European Union.

mcqs.q: The 'Big Four' of Western Europe refers to France, Germany, Italy and:
mcqs.explanation: Western Europe's 'Big Four' historically refers to France, Germany, Italy and the United Kingdom.

mcqs.q: BIMSTEC is an initiative for countries surrounding which body of water?
mcqs.explanation: BIMSTEC (Bangladesh, India, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Thailand, Bhutan, Nepal) is for Bay of Bengal nations.

mcqs.q: Who led the Black Consciousness Movement in South Africa in the 1970s?
mcqs.explanation: Steve Biko was the founder and key figure of the Black Consciousness Movement in 1970s South Africa.

mcqs.q: The acronym BRIC originally stood for Brazil, Russia, India and:
mcqs.explanation: BRIC originally stood for Brazil, Russia, India and China before South Africa joined in 2010.

mcqs.q: BRICS expanded the BRIC group in 2010 to include:
mcqs.explanation: South Africa joined the bloc in 2010, turning BRIC into BRICS.

mcqs.q: Where is BSEC (Black Sea Economic Cooperation) headquartered?
mcqs.explanation: BSEC is headquartered in Istanbul, Turkey.

mcqs.q: Bucharest Nine (B9) is a security group of Eastern European states linked to which alliance?
mcqs.explanation: The Bucharest Nine (B9) is a group of nine NATO members in Eastern Europe formed in 2015.

mcqs.q: Which 1944 arrangement created the IMF and World Bank?
mcqs.explanation: The 1944 Bretton Woods conference created the IMF and the World Bank (IBRD).

mcqs.q: Where is the headquarters of CARICOM (Caribbean Community)?
mcqs.explanation: CARICOM is headquartered in Georgetown, Guyana.

mcqs.q: What was the predecessor to CARICOM (1965–1973)?
mcqs.explanation: CARIFTA (Caribbean Free Trade Association, 1965–1973) was the predecessor of CARICOM.

mcqs.q: CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States) was established in 1991 by:
mcqs.explanation: The CIS was formed in December 1991 by post-Soviet republics after the dissolution of the USSR.

mcqs.q: Which group identifies Colombia, Indonesia, Vietnam, Egypt, Türkiye and South Africa as dynamic emerging markets?
mcqs.explanation: CIVETS = Colombia, Indonesia, Vietnam, Egypt, Türkiye, South Africa — a 'next-tier' emerging-markets grouping.

mcqs.q: What was Comecon (1949–1991)?
mcqs.explanation: Comecon (Council for Mutual Economic Assistance) was a Soviet-led economic organization of the Eastern Bloc, 1949–1991.

mcqs.q: The Commonwealth of Nations consists of 56 independent states, mostly former territories of:
mcqs.explanation: The Commonwealth of Nations comprises 56 mostly former British Empire territories — Pakistan and India included.

mcqs.q: Where is the Council of Europe (COE) headquartered?
mcqs.explanation: The Council of Europe is headquartered in Strasbourg, France.

mcqs.q: The Community for Democracy and Rights of Nations is an alliance of:
mcqs.explanation: It is a small alliance of de facto (partially recognized) states such as Abkhazia, South Ossetia and Transnistria.

mcqs.q: What does the Climate Justice Movement link environmental protection with?
mcqs.explanation: Climate Justice frames environmental protection as inseparable from human-rights and equity concerns.

mcqs.q: CELAC unites 33 countries to promote unity in which region?
mcqs.explanation: CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States) unites 33 countries to promote regional unity excluding the US/Canada.

mcqs.q: The D-8 (Developing Eight) includes Bangladesh, Egypt, Indonesia, Iran, Malaysia, Nigeria, Pakistan and:
mcqs.explanation: The D-8 is a group of 8 developing Muslim-majority economies; the 8th member alongside the others is Türkiye.

mcqs.q: What does the DAC (Development Assistance Committee) do within the OECD?
options: Coordinates donor policies on international aid
mcqs.explanation: The OECD's Development Assistance Committee (DAC) coordinates donor governments' international aid policies.

mcqs.q: The Danube Region Strategy aligns 14 countries for shared infrastructure around which river basin?
mcqs.explanation: The EU's Danube Region Strategy promotes shared infrastructure and policy across the 14-country Danube basin.

mcqs.q: Which mid-20th-century campaign sought political independence for colonized nations?
mcqs.explanation: The Decolonization Movement of the mid-20th century achieved political independence for dozens of colonized nations.

mcqs.q: The Disarmament Movement influenced treaties such as the NPT and:
mcqs.explanation: The Disarmament Movement shaped the Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) and the Comprehensive Test Ban Treaty (CTBT).

mcqs.q: The East African Community (EAC) includes Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi and:
mcqs.explanation: South Sudan joined the EAC in 2016 — making it 6 members at the time (with DRC and Somalia joining later).

mcqs.q: The ECO (Economic Cooperation Organization) was founded in 1985 by Iran, Pakistan and:
mcqs.explanation: ECO was founded in 1985 by Iran, Pakistan and Türkiye as a successor to the older RCD.

mcqs.q: The Eurasian Economic Union (EAEU) includes Russia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan and:
mcqs.explanation: Armenia is the 5th member of the EAEU alongside Russia, Belarus, Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan.

mcqs.q: The European Economic Area (EEA) links EU states with Norway, Iceland and:
mcqs.explanation: The EEA extends the EU single market to Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein.

mcqs.q: Which 2009 treaty institutionalized the European Union (EU)?
mcqs.explanation: The Treaty of Lisbon (signed 2007, in force 1 Dec 2009) institutionalized the modern EU.

mcqs.q: The European Free Trade Association (EFTA) includes Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and:
mcqs.explanation: EFTA's four members are Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland.

mcqs.q: Where is the Eastern Mediterranean Gas Forum (EMGF) headquartered?
mcqs.explanation: The Eastern Mediterranean Gas Forum (EMGF) is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt.

mcqs.q: The Environmental Movement is also commonly known as the:
mcqs.explanation: The Environmental Movement is popularly called the Green Movement.

mcqs.q: Francophonie (OIF) promotes the language and cooperation among nations that speak:
mcqs.explanation: The Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) promotes French language and cultural cooperation across French-speaking nations.

mcqs.q: The Five Eyes (FVEY) is an intelligence alliance of Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the UK and:
mcqs.explanation: Five Eyes (FVEY) = Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the UK and the United States — Anglosphere intelligence sharing.

mcqs.q: The Freedom Movement in India (1857–1947) led to independence from which colonial rule?
mcqs.explanation: India's Freedom Movement (1857–1947) ended British colonial rule and led to independence in 1947.

mcqs.q: The G-7 includes Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the UK and:
mcqs.explanation: G-7 = Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, UK and the United States.

mcqs.q: The G-20 was established in 1999 to coordinate policies regarding:
mcqs.explanation: G-20 was established in 1999 to coordinate international finance and trade after the Asian financial crisis.

mcqs.q: The G-24 represents developing countries within which forums?
mcqs.explanation: The G-24 is a chapter of the G-77 that coordinates developing countries' positions inside the IMF and World Bank.

mcqs.q: The G-77 is a coalition of 134 developing nations formed in 1964 within the:
mcqs.explanation: The G-77 (now 134 members) was formed in 1964 as a coalition of developing countries inside the UN.

mcqs.q: The G-8 was the G-7 expanded to include which country before its suspension in 2014?
mcqs.explanation: The G-7 became G-8 with Russia's accession in 1997; Russia was suspended in 2014 after the Crimea annexation.

mcqs.q: Where is the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) headquartered?
mcqs.explanation: The GCC, founded in 1981, is headquartered in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

mcqs.q: The Global South Movement advocates for equitable representation of which nations?
mcqs.explanation: The Global South Movement seeks fairer representation for developing nations in global governance.

mcqs.q: The Green Climate Movement advocates for sustainable energy transitions and opposes what?
mcqs.explanation: The Green Climate Movement supports renewables and the phase-out of fossil-fuel dominance.

mcqs.q: Which transnational campaign led to institutions like Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch?
mcqs.explanation: The post-WWII Human Rights Movement gave rise to global NGOs like Amnesty International (1961) and Human Rights Watch (1978).

mcqs.q: The IBSA Dialogue Forum is a trilateral grouping of India, Brazil and:
mcqs.explanation: IBSA = India + Brazil + South Africa, three large multi-ethnic democracies of the Global South.

mcqs.q: The Indian Ocean Commission (IOC) links Comoros, Madagascar, Mauritius, Réunion and:
mcqs.explanation: The IOC unites five Indian Ocean island states: Comoros, Madagascar, Mauritius, Réunion (France) and Seychelles.

mcqs.q: The I2U2 Group is a strategic partnership among India, Israel, the US and:
mcqs.explanation: I2U2 (the 'West Asian Quad') links India, Israel, the United States and the UAE.

mcqs.q: The International Labour Movement shaped the framework of which organization?
mcqs.explanation: The labour movement led to the founding of the International Labour Organization (ILO) in 1919.

mcqs.q: The Inter-American System encompasses institutions supporting democracy in the Americas, such as the:
mcqs.explanation: The OAS is the principal political body of the Inter-American System.

mcqs.q: The Islamic Revival Movement promotes the socio-political rejuvenation of:
mcqs.explanation: The Islamic Revival Movement promotes a renewal of Islamic socio-political values across the Muslim world.

mcqs.q: JUSCANZ is a coordination group of non-EU democracies including Japan, US, Canada, Australia and:
mcqs.explanation: JUSCANZ = Japan, US, Canada, Australia and New Zealand — non-EU developed democracies inside the UN.

mcqs.q: The Japan-ASEAN Partnership was established in 1977 to emphasize trade, security and:
mcqs.explanation: Japan-ASEAN cooperation has emphasised trade, security and regional infrastructure since 1977.

mcqs.q: The Joint Arab Economic Action Council (JAEAC) is an economic institution under the:
mcqs.explanation: JAEAC operates under the Arab League to coordinate Arab economic policy.

mcqs.q: The Justice and Development Movement is notably associated with political parties in Morocco and:
mcqs.explanation: Justice & Development Parties are major political forces in Morocco (PJD) and Türkiye (AKP).

mcqs.q: The Kimberley Process Certification Scheme (KPCS) aims to prevent the trade in:
mcqs.explanation: The KPCS, launched in 2003, certifies that rough diamonds are not 'conflict diamonds' funding civil wars.

mcqs.q: The Kigali Amendment Coalition refers to the agreement under the Montreal Protocol to phase down:
mcqs.explanation: The 2016 Kigali Amendment to the Montreal Protocol phases down HFC refrigerants — potent greenhouse gases.

mcqs.q: The Kuwait Declaration Group is a cooperative forum among Gulf and Arab states to address:
mcqs.explanation: The Kuwait Declaration Group addresses Gulf and Arab security and humanitarian priorities.

mcqs.q: The Kashmir Solidarity Movement is observed annually on:
mcqs.explanation: Kashmir Solidarity Day is observed annually on 5 February in Pakistan in support of Kashmiri self-determination.

mcqs.q: LATAM denotes the geopolitical and economic region covering:
mcqs.explanation: LATAM is shorthand for Latin America — Central and South America.

mcqs.q: LATCAR emphasizes cultural and economic collaboration between Latin America and:
mcqs.explanation: LATCAR is a regional label tying Latin America with the Caribbean.

mcqs.q: The Levant refers to the subregion containing Cyprus, Israel, Jordan, Lebanon, Palestine and:
mcqs.explanation: The Levant comprises Cyprus, Israel, Jordan, Lebanon, Palestine and Syria — the eastern Mediterranean.

mcqs.q: Founded in 1945, which is the main political organization uniting Arab nations?
mcqs.explanation: The Arab League (League of Arab States) was founded in Cairo in 1945.

mcqs.q: The LDC Group within the UN and WTO represents the world's poorest economies to advocate for:
mcqs.explanation: The LDC Group represents the least-developed countries to push for fair trade rules and debt relief.

mcqs.q: Liberation Movements refer to struggles for independence, such as the ANC in South Africa and the PLO in:
mcqs.explanation: The PLO (Palestine Liberation Organization) is the parallel example of liberation movements in the Palestinian context.

mcqs.q: The Landless Peasants' Movement (MST) advocating land reform was founded in 1984 in:
mcqs.explanation: Brazil's Movimento dos Trabalhadores Rurais Sem Terra (MST) was founded in 1984 to advocate land reform.

mcqs.q: The League Against Imperialism was established in 1927 in which city?
mcqs.explanation: The League Against Imperialism was established at a congress in Brussels in 1927.

mcqs.q: Mercosur (Southern Common Market) originally included Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay and:
mcqs.explanation: Mercosur's founding members in 1991 were Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay.

mcqs.q: MIKTA is an informal partnership of Mexico, Indonesia, South Korea, Türkiye and:
mcqs.explanation: MIKTA = Mexico, Indonesia, South Korea, Türkiye, Australia — five middle-power democracies.

mcqs.q: The geopolitical term MENA encompasses Arab and Muslim-majority countries from Morocco to:
mcqs.explanation: MENA = Middle East & North Africa — stretches from Morocco in the west to Iran in the east.

mcqs.q: The term MEP recognizes the inclusion of which country in Middle Eastern cooperation frameworks?
mcqs.explanation: MEP (Middle East + Pakistan) is a regional designation that brackets Pakistan with Middle Eastern cooperation frameworks.

mcqs.q: The Mediterranean Dialogue (MD) is a cooperation forum launched in 1994 by:
mcqs.explanation: NATO's Mediterranean Dialogue (1994) is a cooperation forum with non-NATO Mediterranean states.

mcqs.q: The Melanesian Spearhead Group (MSG) unites Fiji, Papua New Guinea, Solomon Islands and:
mcqs.explanation: The MSG includes Fiji, Papua New Guinea, Solomon Islands and Vanuatu (plus FLNKS observer for Kanaks).

mcqs.q: The Middle Powers Movement refers to diplomatic efforts by medium-sized states such as Canada, Australia, South Korea and:
mcqs.explanation: Türkiye is often grouped with Canada, Australia and South Korea as a leading 'middle power' in global diplomacy.

mcqs.q: The Moral Re-Armament Movement (MRA) later evolved into:
mcqs.explanation: The Moral Re-Armament Movement re-branded as 'Initiatives of Change International'.

mcqs.q: The Muslim Brotherhood Movement was founded in 1928 in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Muslim Brotherhood was founded in Egypt by Hassan al-Banna in 1928.

mcqs.q: The Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) was formed in 1961 to unite countries choosing neutrality during the:
mcqs.explanation: NAM was founded in 1961 in Belgrade to unite states staying neutral between the US and Soviet blocs in the Cold War.

mcqs.q: Where is NATO (North Atlantic Treaty Organization) headquartered?
mcqs.explanation: NATO is headquartered in Brussels, Belgium.

mcqs.q: NAFTA was replaced in 2020 by which agreement?
mcqs.explanation: NAFTA (1994) was replaced by USMCA (US-Mexico-Canada Agreement) on 1 July 2020.

mcqs.q: The Nordic Council fosters cooperation among Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Iceland and:
mcqs.explanation: The Nordic Council unites Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Iceland and Finland.

mcqs.q: The Nordic-Baltic Eight (NB8) is a regional alliance cooperating on energy, digitalization and:
mcqs.explanation: NB8 cooperates on energy, digitalization and defence — particularly important since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

mcqs.q: The 'Next Eleven' (N-11) is a term identifying 11 emerging economies coined by:
mcqs.explanation: Goldman Sachs coined the 'Next Eleven' (N-11) — emerging economies expected to follow BRICs.

mcqs.q: The Nasserist Movement was inspired by which Egyptian leader?
mcqs.explanation: Nasserism — pan-Arab socialism — was inspired by Egyptian President Gamal Abdel Nasser.

mcqs.q: The Nuclear Disarmament Movement includes campaigns like the CND advocating the abolition of:
mcqs.explanation: CND (Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament) is the most famous of the nuclear-disarmament campaigns.

mcqs.q: The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), founded in 1969, is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: The OIC is headquartered in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia. Pakistan is a founding member.

mcqs.q: The Organization of American States (OAS) is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: The OAS is headquartered in Washington, D.C., USA.

mcqs.q: Where is the OECD headquartered?
mcqs.explanation: The OECD (Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development) is headquartered in Paris.

mcqs.q: The Organization of Eastern Caribbean States (OECS) promotes economic and judicial integration among small island nations in the:
mcqs.explanation: The OECS unites 11 small island states of the Eastern Caribbean.

mcqs.q: The Organization of Ibero-American States (OIAS) unites countries speaking Spanish and:
mcqs.explanation: OIAS unites Spanish- and Portuguese-speaking Ibero-American nations.

mcqs.q: The Organization of Turkic States (OTS) unites Türkiye, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan and:
mcqs.explanation: OTS members: Türkiye, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan and Uzbekistan.

mcqs.q: Where is OPEC headquartered despite being founded in Baghdad?
mcqs.explanation: OPEC was founded in Baghdad in 1960 but is headquartered in Vienna, Austria since 1965.

mcqs.q: OPEC Plus (OPEC+) extends cooperation to include non-member oil producers like:
mcqs.explanation: OPEC+ adds non-OPEC producers such as Russia to coordinate oil-output policy.

mcqs.q: What does the OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe) focus on?
mcqs.explanation: OSCE focuses on European peace, security, arms control and democracy.

mcqs.q: The Open Balkan Initiative was launched in 2019 by Albania, North Macedonia and:
mcqs.explanation: Open Balkan was launched in 2019 by Albania, North Macedonia and Serbia.

mcqs.q: The Oppressed Nations Movement is a solidarity campaign supporting anti-imperial causes across Asia, Africa and:
mcqs.explanation: The Oppressed Nations Movement extends solidarity across Asia, Africa and Latin America.

mcqs.q: The P5 (Permanent Five) members of the UN Security Council are China, France, Russia, the UK and:
mcqs.explanation: The P5 are the five permanent UNSC members with veto power: China, France, Russia, UK and US.

mcqs.q: The Pacific Alliance trade bloc joins Chile, Colombia, Mexico and:
mcqs.explanation: The Pacific Alliance is a Latin American trade bloc of Chile, Colombia, Mexico and Peru.

mcqs.q: Which ideological movement advocated unity and independence of African peoples, shaping the African Union?
mcqs.explanation: The Pan-African Movement advocated African unity and independence and inspired the OAU/AU.

mcqs.q: Which movement promoted political and cultural unity of Arab nations and opposition to Western colonialism?
mcqs.explanation: The Pan-Arab Movement promoted Arab political and cultural unity against Western colonial rule.

mcqs.q: The PLO was founded in 1964 as a nationalist movement for:
mcqs.explanation: The Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) was founded in 1964 to pursue Palestinian statehood.

mcqs.q: The Paris Club is an informal group of major creditor nations that coordinate:
mcqs.explanation: The Paris Club is an informal group of creditor states that negotiate debt relief for indebted countries.

mcqs.q: The Pacific Islands Forum (PIF) promotes political and climate cooperation among how many Pacific nations?
mcqs.explanation: The Pacific Islands Forum has 18 Pacific member states.

mcqs.q: The Pan-European Movement initiated in 1923 advocated European unity and the eventual creation of the:
mcqs.explanation: The Pan-European Movement (1923, Coudenhove-Kalergi) helped lay the intellectual groundwork for the EU.

mcqs.q: The People's Solidarity Movement promotes social justice, equality and:
mcqs.explanation: The People's Solidarity Movement promotes social justice, equality and anti-globalisation.

mcqs.q: The QUAD (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) includes Australia, India, Japan and the:
mcqs.explanation: The QUAD = Australia, India, Japan and the United States — an Indo-Pacific security dialogue.

mcqs.q: QIZ (Qualified Industrial Zones) are special economic areas established under US agreements with Egypt and:
mcqs.explanation: Qualified Industrial Zones (QIZ) were established under US agreements with Jordan and Egypt to allow duty-free access to the US.

mcqs.q: The Qatar-Turkey Supreme Strategic Committee was created in 2014 to coordinate:
mcqs.explanation: The Qatar-Turkey Supreme Strategic Committee coordinates security, investment and diplomacy.

mcqs.q: The Quint Group is an informal diplomatic coalition of the US, UK, France, Germany and:
mcqs.explanation: The Quint = US + UK + France + Germany + Italy — an informal Western diplomatic coordination group.

mcqs.q: The Quebec Sovereignty Movement advocates independence for the province of Quebec in which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Quebec Sovereignty Movement seeks Quebec's independence from Canada.

mcqs.q: RCEP is the world's largest trade bloc involving ASEAN, China, Japan, South Korea, Australia and:
mcqs.explanation: RCEP (Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership) — the world's largest trade bloc — includes New Zealand.

mcqs.q: The Rio Group was a political cooperation forum of Latin American states succeeded by:
mcqs.explanation: The Rio Group was succeeded by CELAC in 2010.

mcqs.q: RATS (Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure) is a permanent body of which organization, based in Tashkent?
mcqs.explanation: RATS is the SCO's permanent anti-terrorism body, based in Tashkent, Uzbekistan.

mcqs.q: RIMES provides early-warning data for natural disasters in Asia-Pacific and is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: RIMES (Regional Integrated Multi-Hazard Early Warning System) is headquartered in Bangkok.

mcqs.q: The Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement was founded in 1863 by:
mcqs.explanation: Henry Dunant founded the Red Cross in 1863 in Geneva.

mcqs.q: The Refugee Rights Movement is a humanitarian campaign that shapes the policy of which UN agency?
mcqs.explanation: The Refugee Rights Movement shapes the policy framework of the UNHCR.

mcqs.q: The Riyadh Declaration Cooperation Framework refers to Gulf-Arab coordination initiatives under the leadership of:
mcqs.explanation: Saudi Arabia leads the Riyadh Declaration Cooperation Framework on Gulf-Arab coordination.

mcqs.q: Revolutionary Movements (Global) refer to struggles that reshaped geopolitics, such as the Bolshevik Revolution, Chinese Revolution and:
mcqs.explanation: The Cuban Revolution (1959) joins the Bolshevik (1917) and Chinese (1949) revolutions among modern world-shaping events.

mcqs.q: SAARC is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: SAARC (South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation) is headquartered in Kathmandu, Nepal.

mcqs.q: SADC (Southern African Development Community) is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: SADC is headquartered in Gaborone, Botswana.

mcqs.q: The SCO (Shanghai Cooperation Organization) secretariat is in:
mcqs.explanation: The SCO's permanent secretariat is in Beijing; its anti-terror RATS is in Tashkent.

mcqs.q: SEATO was a defense alliance created in 1954 to contain communism in Asia and was dissolved in:
mcqs.explanation: SEATO (Southeast Asia Treaty Organization, 1954) was dissolved in 1977.

mcqs.q: The Solidarity Movement opposing communist rule in Poland was led by:
mcqs.explanation: Lech Wałęsa led Poland's Solidarity (Solidarność) trade-union movement against communist rule.

mcqs.q: The Sufi Revival Movement emphasizes spiritual purification and peace through traditional Islamic:
mcqs.explanation: The Sufi Revival Movement emphasises traditional Islamic mysticism and inner spiritual purification.

mcqs.q: The Silk Road Economic Belt is part of which country's 2013 Belt and Road Initiative (BRI)?
mcqs.explanation: China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI) was launched in 2013 — the Silk Road Economic Belt is its overland component.

mcqs.q: Socialist International (SI) unites social-democratic parties worldwide and is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: Socialist International is headquartered in London.

mcqs.q: The Summit of the Future is a 2024–2025 initiative by which organization focusing on global governance reform?
mcqs.explanation: The UN's Summit of the Future (Sept 2024) focuses on multilateral governance reform.

mcqs.q: The South-South Cooperation Movement encouraged developing countries to collaborate, leading to the creation of the G-77 and:
mcqs.explanation: South-South Cooperation gave rise to both the G-77 (1964) and the Non-Aligned Movement (NAM, 1961).

mcqs.q: ZOPACAS is a UN-endorsed initiative established in 1986 to prevent the militarization of the:
mcqs.explanation: ZOPACAS (Zone of Peace and Cooperation of the South Atlantic) was established by UNGA Resolution 41/11 in 1986.

mcqs.q: The Scandinavian Countries (Nordics) are recognized for strong welfare models and neutrality. They include Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Finland and:
mcqs.explanation: The Nordics include Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Finland and Iceland.

mcqs.q: The Sustainable Development Movement arose globally from the 1992 Earth Summit held in:
mcqs.explanation: The 1992 Earth Summit was held in Rio de Janeiro, launching the global sustainable-development agenda.

mcqs.q: The Transatlantic Alliance refers to the strategic partnership between North America and Europe primarily through:
mcqs.explanation: NATO is the primary institutional embodiment of the Transatlantic Alliance.

mcqs.q: The Trans-Pacific Partnership (TPP) was restructured as the CPTPP after which country withdrew in 2017?
mcqs.explanation: The US withdrew from TPP in 2017; the remaining 11 nations re-signed it as the CPTPP in 2018.

mcqs.q: The Tiger Cub Economies refer to five rapidly growing economies: Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam and:
mcqs.explanation: Tiger Cub Economies = Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam, Philippines — the next generation after the original Asian Tigers.

mcqs.q: The Treaty of Amity and Cooperation (TAC) was signed in 1976 under which organization?
mcqs.explanation: The Treaty of Amity and Cooperation (TAC) was signed in 1976 under ASEAN as its founding political document.

mcqs.q: The Third World Movement refers to the activism of post-colonial nations advocating for justice during the:
mcqs.explanation: The Third World Movement mobilised post-colonial nations during the Cold War era.

mcqs.q: The Turkic Cultural Movement is formalized under the:
mcqs.explanation: Turkic cultural and political cooperation is formalised under the Organization of Turkic States (OTS).

mcqs.q: The Two-State Solution Movement advocates for an independent State of Palestine alongside:
mcqs.explanation: The Two-State Solution envisions an independent Palestine alongside Israel.

mcqs.q: The Tahrir Square Movement was a mass pro-democracy movement that began in 2011 in:
mcqs.explanation: The Tahrir Square Movement in Cairo was the heart of Egypt's 2011 Arab Spring uprising.

mcqs.q: The United Nations (UN) was founded in 1945 and currently has how many member states?
mcqs.explanation: The UN currently has 193 member states (South Sudan being the newest, joined 2011).

mcqs.q: UNESCO is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: UNESCO is headquartered in Paris.

mcqs.q: UNICEF provides humanitarian and developmental assistance to whom?
mcqs.explanation: UNICEF is the UN agency dedicated to children's welfare worldwide.

mcqs.q: UNHCR is the UN agency mandated to protect:
mcqs.explanation: The UN High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) protects refugees and stateless persons.

mcqs.q: UNEP (United Nations Environment Programme) is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: UNEP is the only major UN agency headquartered in Africa — Nairobi, Kenya.

mcqs.q: UNDP was formed in 1965 to promote sustainable development and:
mcqs.explanation: UNDP (UN Development Programme) was formed in 1965 to promote sustainable development and poverty eradication.

mcqs.q: The Union for the Mediterranean (UfM) is headquartered in:
mcqs.explanation: The Union for the Mediterranean is headquartered in Barcelona, Spain.

mcqs.q: The Universal Postal Union (UPU) coordinates international mail standards and is based in:
mcqs.explanation: The UPU — one of the oldest specialised UN agencies — is based in Bern, Switzerland.

mcqs.q: UNOOSA promotes the peaceful and sustainable use of:
mcqs.explanation: UNOOSA (UN Office for Outer Space Affairs) coordinates peaceful and sustainable use of outer space.

mcqs.q: UN-Women was established in 2010 to promote:
mcqs.explanation: UN Women was established in 2010 to promote gender equality and the empowerment of women.

mcqs.q: UNIDO was founded in 1966 to accelerate what in developing countries?
mcqs.explanation: UNIDO (UN Industrial Development Organization) was founded in 1966 to accelerate industrialization in the developing world.

mcqs.q: UNCTAD integrates developing countries into the global economy through:
mcqs.explanation: UNCTAD (UN Conference on Trade and Development) integrates developing countries into the global economy through fair trade and investment policy.

mcqs.q: The UAE-Israel Abraham Accords were signed in what year?
mcqs.explanation: The Abraham Accords normalising UAE-Israel relations were signed in Washington, D.C. on 15 September 2020.

mcqs.q: The Ummah Movement seeks the unity and revival of the global:
mcqs.explanation: The Ummah Movement seeks the unity and revival of the global Muslim community.

mcqs.q: The Universal Human Rights Movement led to the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in:
mcqs.explanation: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights was adopted by the UN General Assembly on 10 December 1948.

mcqs.q: The V4 (Visegrád Group) includes Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland and:
mcqs.explanation: V4 = Czech Republic, Hungary, Poland and Slovakia.

mcqs.q: VISTA refers to five emerging economies identified in 2006: Vietnam, Indonesia, South Africa, Türkiye and:
mcqs.explanation: VISTA = Vietnam, Indonesia, South Africa, Türkiye, Argentina — coined by Kenichi Ohmae in 2006.

mcqs.q: The Vienna Convention Framework regulates treaties, diplomatic relations and:
mcqs.explanation: Vienna Conventions cover treaties, diplomatic & consular relations and environmental law (e.g. ozone layer).

mcqs.q: The Voluntary Service Movement includes global initiatives like VSO (UK) and the:
mcqs.explanation: Voluntary Service Overseas (VSO, UK) and the Peace Corps (USA) are flagship voluntary-service initiatives.

mcqs.q: The Voice of the Global South Summit is an initiative led since 2023 by which country?
mcqs.explanation: India launched the Voice of the Global South Summit in January 2023.

mcqs.q: The Vietnam Solidarity Movement refers to global protests in the 1960s opposing the:
mcqs.explanation: Global Vietnam Solidarity protests in the 1960s opposed the US-led Vietnam War.

mcqs.q: The Vatican and Holy See Diplomacy Network represents the global diplomatic outreach of:
mcqs.explanation: Vatican diplomacy is the diplomatic arm of the Vatican City State (Holy See).

mcqs.q: The Victims' Rights Movement is supported by UN agencies and the:
mcqs.explanation: The Victims' Rights Movement is supported by UN agencies and the International Criminal Court (ICC).
